examples: A blood clot in a vein is called a venous thromboembolism (or VTE).
The 2001 ACC/AHA/ESC AF guidelines recommend anticoagulation for patients considered at medium or high risk of thromboembolism.
The second study in the journal looked at 36 published research papers dealing with medications used to prevent venous thromboembolism.
The recent arrival of novel oral anticoagulants has provided important new options for venous thromboembolism (VTE) treatment and prevention.
The label now includes a black box warning that it can increase the risk of death, MI, stroke, venous thromboembolism, thrombosis of vascular access, and tumor progression or recurrence.
